regimeNotes: NEUTRAL regime maintained 2026-06-21 (Sunday). Stock prices held at June 18 close — June 19 was Juneteenth market holiday; June 20-21 are weekend; next trading day is Monday June 22. SPY $746.74, NVDA $210.69, MSFT $379.40, GOOGL $368.03, UNH $400.96 (all unchanged since last review). Crypto recovering modestly: BTC $64,238 (+2.77% from $62,504 on June 19), ETH $1,739.73 (+3.07%), SOL $73.28 (+7.48%). F&G index improved to 23 (still Extreme Fear, up from 14 on June 19). BTC dominance 56.22%, total crypto mcap $2.29T (+1.03% 24h). 10Y yield 4.45% (elevated, restrictive). DXY 100.85 (slightly firmer vs ~99 on June 19, mild headwind for risk). EUR/USD 1.1467 (slightly weaker USD vs 1.165 prior). Fed: hawkish stance maintained, no cuts priced near-term. HOLD day — 0 trades. Crypto sleeve at 20.05% NAV (at neutral 20% cap — no room to add). All stock positions near 15% NAV entry cap at ATH = not a fear-driven entry. Cash 13.23% NAV. All theses and invalidation levels intact. NAV $100,130.91 (+$617 vs June 19, driven by crypto recovery). Next catalysts: PCE data June 25, first trading day back Monday June 22.

SPY.thesis: Core US equity beta ($746.74, 13.07% NAV). S&P near all-time highs with AI/semiconductor rally sustaining broad index. Diversified anchor under the growth-tilted mandate; participates in any continued risk-on leg.
SPY.invalidationLevel: Sustained close below 660 USD or confirmed Fed re-pricing toward multiple hikes pushing SPY into a technical downtrend.

NVDA.thesis: AI/compute secular leader at $210.69 (above $208.44 avgCost, ~14.64% NAV). Data-center revenue and hyperscaler capex still accelerating. Highest-conviction growth driver for the 33% CAGR target.
NVDA.invalidationLevel: AI capex digestion or data-center demand rollover; close below ~150 USD.

MSFT.thesis: Quality AI/cloud compounder at $379.40 (below $391.98 avgCost, ~14.43% NAV). Broad market conditions affecting quality names; cloud+AI thesis intact and FCF durable. Watching for recovery toward 390+.
MSFT.invalidationLevel: Cloud growth deceleration or AI capex without demonstrable ROI; close below 340 USD.

GOOGL.thesis: Quality growth (search + cloud + AI) at $368.03 (above $366.53 avgCost, ~14.44% NAV). Near-monopoly search funds AI optionality; resilient earnings in hawkish-rate tape.
GOOGL.invalidationLevel: Search share erosion to AI rivals; close below 310 USD.

UNH.thesis: Defensive value at $400.96 (below $408.52 avgCost, ~5.88% NAV). Medical-cost and regulatory headwinds persist; retained as defensive ballast. Analyst targets 450-466 USD give upside.
UNH.invalidationLevel: Further regulatory or medical-cost shock; close below 360 USD.

GLD.thesis: Tail/debasement hedge at $387.12 (above $386.54 avgCost, ~4.27% NAV). Central-bank buying supportive; small size prevents drag on 33% CAGR target. DXY firming slightly (100.85) bears watching.
GLD.invalidationLevel: Real yields spike with a strong USD reversal; close below 350 USD.

BTC.thesis: Core crypto sleeve leader at $64,238 (below $64,629 avgCost, ~14.39% NAV). F&G recovering to 23 (Extreme Fear still), BTC dominance 56.22% stable. Recovery from $62,504 confirms support zone. Positioned for the next risk-on expansion to 35% sleeve cap.

ETH.thesis: Discounted L1 leader at $1,739.73 (above $1,667.47 avgCost, ~4.17% NAV). Dominance 9.17% remains historically low = asymmetric upside when risk-on returns. Held within the 20% crypto sleeve.
ETH.invalidationLevel: Structural loss of share to L1 rivals; close below 1,300 USD.

SOL.thesis: High-beta satellite at $73.28 (above $67.81 avgCost, ~1.49% NAV). +7.48% recovery today shows strong beta to crypto recovery. First position to scale up when sleeve cap rises to 35% on a risk-on regime flip.
SOL.invalidationLevel: Break below 52 USD; network deterioration or sentiment collapse.
